title: Debt statements in MSCA - Canada.ca
content:
## Debt statements in MSCA
- How to receive your debt statements
- How to view your debt statements
- How to stop receiving your debt  statement notifications

## How to receive your debt statements
Your debt statements are available by default in your My  Service Canada Account (MSCA) inbox for the following programs:
- Canada  Apprentice Loan
- Canada  Education Savings Program
- Canada  Pension Plan (CPP)
- Canada  Student Loans (CSL)
- Employment  Insurance (EI)
- Grants  & Contributions (G&C)
- Old  Age Security (OAS)
- Operations  & Maintenance (O&M) debts
- Training  Allowance Payment System (TAPS)
- Temporary  Foreign Worker Program
- Wage  Earner Protection Program (WEPP)
You can choose to receive an email notification instead of  paper mail when a new debt statement is available by following these steps:
- Sign in or register for MSCA
- If you have not already added your email address, follow the on-screen instructions
- On the “Inbox notification preferences” page, select “Email notification only”
Once this step is complete, you will receive an email when a  new debt statement is available in your MSCA inbox. You can change your  notification preferences anytime by selecting “Profile and  Preferences” under the “Account” menu on your MSCA dashboard.
Register or Sign in to MSCA

## How to view your debt statements
To view your debt statements, select “Inbox” at the top of  your MSCA dashboard.

## How to stop receiving your debt statement notifications
To stop receiving email notifications for your debt  statement:
- Go to the “Account” menu located at the top of  your MSCA dashboard
- Select “Profile and preferences”
- Select “Inbox notification preferences”
Once this step is complete, your debt statements will still  appear in your inbox, but you’ll no longer receive email notifications about  it. Instead, a copy of the debt statement will be mailed to you.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
